context: Tests whether the agent uses the --record and --record-path flags correctly to produce an asciicast v2 recording, uses --json flag, follows daemon lifecycle, and properly kills the session to finalize the recording.

checklist.name: Uses --json flag
checklist.description: The `virtui run` command includes --json or -j flag

checklist.name: Daemon status check
checklist.description: Script checks daemon status with `virtui daemon status` before creating any session

checklist.name: Daemon started if needed
checklist.description: Script conditionally starts the daemon with `virtui daemon start` if not running

checklist.name: --record flag used
checklist.description: The `virtui run` command includes the `--record` flag to enable session recording

checklist.name: --record-path set correctly
checklist.description: The `--record-path` flag is used and set to `./demo/onboarding.cast` (or equivalent relative path that results in that location)

checklist.name: Output directory created
checklist.description: Script creates the `demo/` directory before running virtui (e.g. via `mkdir -p demo`)

checklist.name: Session ID captured
checklist.description: The session_id from the `run` command is captured and used in subsequent commands

checklist.name: Session killed to finalize
checklist.description: Script kills the session with `virtui kill <session_id>` — required to finalize/stop the recording

checklist.name: Cast file exists
checklist.description: The file `demo/onboarding.cast` exists after the script runs

checklist.name: Daemon stopped
checklist.description: Script runs `virtui daemon stop` after all work is complete

checklist.name: Pipeline or type+press used
checklist.description: Interaction commands use pipeline or type+press steps rather than bare exec (to ensure Enter is sent reliably)
